findings.title: Starlette — Denial of Service via unbounded multipart form field accumulation
findings.description: Starlette versions prior to 0.40.0 did not enforce a maximum number of fields when parsing multipart form data, allowing a remote unauthenticated attacker to cause excessive memory consumption and denial of service by sending a request with a large number of small form fields.
findings.status: PATCHED — installed version 0.45.3 is above the fixed version

findings.title: python-multipart — Denial of Service via crafted multipart boundary
findings.description: python-multipart < 0.0.19 is vulnerable to a denial-of-service attack. A specially crafted Content-Type header with an extremely long boundary parameter causes the parser to consume excessive CPU/memory.
findings.status: PATCHED — installed version 0.0.22 is above the fixed version

findings.title: ReportLab — Remote Code Execution via malicious SVG/HTML input
findings.description: ReportLab < 3.6.13 is vulnerable to remote code execution via a specially crafted color attribute in an SVG or paragraph style string, allowing arbitrary Python code execution through the eval() function.
findings.status: PATCHED — installed version 4.4.10 is well above the fixed version
findings.note: CAUTION: Although patched, the PDF export endpoint (pdf_export.py) should be reviewed to confirm no user-controlled input is ever passed as ReportLab style/SVG strings.

findings.title: Mako — Potential XSS via unescaped template rendering
findings.description: Mako templates do not auto-escape HTML by default. Applications rendering user-controlled data through Mako templates without explicit escaping are vulnerable to XSS. This applies to Alembic migration script templates if they are exposed to user input — generally not a concern for migration scripts but noted for completeness.
findings.status: RISK ACCEPTED — Alembic uses Mako only for migration template generation, not for rendering user data

findings.title: h11 — HTTP request smuggling via lenient header parsing
findings.description: h11 versions prior to 0.16.0 may accept malformed HTTP headers in a way that could enable HTTP request smuggling when deployed behind certain reverse proxies or load balancers that parse headers differently.
findings.status: POTENTIALLY AFFECTED — installed version 0.14.0 is below the fixed version. Assess exposure based on reverse proxy configuration.
findings.remediation: Upgrade uvicorn to a version that pulls in h11 >= 0.16.0. Run: pip install 'uvicorn[standard]>=0.32.0' and verify h11 resolves to >= 0.16.0.

notes: Versions for transitive dependencies were inferred from known compatibility matrices as of 2026-03-06; pin all transitive deps in a lock file (pip-compile or uv) for deterministic supply chain control.

The application uses a custom pure-Python JWT implementation (app/jwt_utils.py) instead of PyJWT or python-jose. This avoids algorithm-confusion CVEs present in those libraries but means the implementation has not received the same security scrutiny as widely-audited libraries.
No lock file (requirements.txt.lock or similar) is present. Add pip-compile or uv lock to CI to prevent silent transitive dependency upgrades.
